title: Accessibility in Frontend Frameworks Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of semantic HTML, Aria roles/labels, and keyboard navigation management (Focus Rings).

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily reason to use a native <button> tag instead of a <div> for a clickable element?
options: To provide built-in semantic meaning, keyboard accessibility, and support for screen readers.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what attribute would you use on a button with only an icon (e.g., a 'close' X button) to provide text for screen readers?
Is it important to manage 'focus rings' as part of an accessible web application design?
options: Yes, as they allow keyboard users to see which element is currently selected.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the key component of a logical 'Tab Order' in a professional application?
options: A random order to keep users engaged.
A logical, predictable sequence as the user presses 'Tab' to move through interactive elements.

questions.question: Modern frameworks like Tailwind provides specific utilities (e.g., focus:ring) specifically for managing accessibility states.
